keyTerms.General Tso's chicken: A sweet, deep-fried chicken dish ubiquitous in American Chinese restaurants but virtually unknown in China
keyTerms.fortune cookie: A crisp cookie containing a paper fortune, invented in California — likely by Japanese immigrants, not Chinese
keyTerms.chop suey: A dish whose name means 'odds and ends,' created by Chinese railroad cooks from whatever ingredients were available
keyTerms.wok hei: The 'breath of the wok' — the smoky, high-heat searing that defines authentic stir-fry, preserved even in adapted cuisines
keyTerms.culinary translation: The process by which immigrant cuisines adapt to local ingredients and tastes while retaining core principles
